gloss.en: to affect someone so strongly they cannot react

words.example: She was overwhelmed by feelings of guilt.

gloss.en: very great or strong; impossible to resist

words.example: The evidence against him was overwhelming.

gloss.en: a thick piece of soft material for cleaning or protecting

words.example: She used a cotton pad to clean her face.

gloss.en: something that limits how something can be done

words.example: We must work within the parameters of the budget.

gloss.en: connected with a parent or parents

words.example: Young children need parental support.

gloss.en: an area with its own church and priest

words.example: The parish church is over 500 years old.

gloss.en: connected with a parliament

words.example: The country held parliamentary elections last year.

gloss.en: not complete or whole

words.example: It was only a partial solution to the problem.

gloss.en: partly; not completely

words.example: The road was partially blocked by a fallen tree.

gloss.en: the process of time going by

words.example: The wood darkens with the passing of time.

gloss.en: accepting things without trying to change them

words.example: He played a passive role in the relationship.

gloss.en: a minister in charge of a Christian church

words.example: The pastor led the Sunday service.

gloss.en: a small area different from what surrounds it

words.example: The dog had a white patch on its back.

gloss.en: official right to be the only maker of an invention

words.example: She applied for a patent on her new invention.

gloss.en: a track that serves as a path

words.example: They walked along a narrow pathway through the woods.

gloss.en: act of moving around an area to prevent trouble

words.example: Security guards make regular patrols at night.

gloss.en: a person who supports artists with money

words.example: He was a generous patron of many artists.

gloss.en: the point when something is strongest or best

words.example: Traffic reaches its peak between 8 and 9.

gloss.en: a farmer who owns or rents a small plot

words.example: The land was farmed by poor peasants.

gloss.en: strange or unusual, often in an unpleasant way

words.example: There was a peculiar smell in the room.

gloss.en: to continue despite difficulties or opposition

words.example: Why do you persist in blaming yourself?

gloss.en: determined to continue despite difficulties

words.example: He is a persistent salesman who never gives up.

gloss.en: the people who work for an organization

words.example: There is a severe shortage of skilled personnel.

gloss.en: a signed document asking authority to change something

words.example: Would you like to sign our petition?

gloss.en: a person who studies or writes about philosophy

words.example: We studied the Greek philosopher Aristotle.

gloss.en: connected with philosophy

words.example: We read the philosophical writings of Kant.

gloss.en: a doctor, especially in general medicine

words.example: She is a family physician in Atlanta.

gloss.en: the first person to develop an area of knowledge

words.example: She was a pioneer in the field of microsurgery.

gloss.en: a series of pipes carrying oil or gas long distances

words.example: They plan to lay a gas pipeline through the region.

gloss.en: a person who attacks ships at sea to steal

words.example: A pirate ship came looking for treasure.

gloss.en: a large deep hole in the ground

words.example: We dug a deep pit in the yard.

gloss.en: a serious emotional request for urgent action

words.example: She made an impassioned plea for help.

gloss.en: to ask for something strongly and seriously

words.example: She pleaded with him not to go.

gloss.en: a serious promise

words.example: The party made a pledge of support.

gloss.en: a part that connects equipment to the electricity supply

words.example: The iron is fitted with a three-pin plug.

gloss.en: to move suddenly forwards or downwards

words.example: She lost her balance and plunged into the river.

gloss.en: a long thin straight piece of wood or metal

words.example: He fixed the tent pole into the ground.

gloss.en: process of questioning people to learn general opinion

words.example: They decided to conduct a poll on the issue.

gloss.en: a small area of still water, often artificial

words.example: There are fish swimming in the pond.

gloss.en: connected with modern popular music

words.example: She wrote a catchy pop song.

gloss.en: a flat case for carrying documents or drawings

words.example: I left my portfolio on the train.

gloss.en: to show or describe someone in art or writing

words.example: The painting portrays the duke's wife.

gloss.en: happening in the period after a war

words.example: The post-war years brought rapid change.

gloss.en: to arrange for something to happen later

words.example: The game has been postponed three times.

gloss.en: a person who works in a profession like medicine

words.example: She is a dental practitioner in town.

gloss.en: to give a religious talk, especially in church

words.example: She preached to the congregation about forgiveness.

gloss.en: a past decision used as an example to follow

words.example: The ruling set a precedent for future cases.

gloss.en: the quality of being exact and accurate

words.example: Her writing is imaginative but lacks precision.

gloss.en: an animal that kills and eats other animals

words.example: Some animals have no natural predators.

gloss.en: a person who held a job before someone else

words.example: He reversed the policies of his predecessor.

gloss.en: mostly; mainly

words.example: She works in a predominantly male environment.

gloss.en: the state of being pregnant

words.example: She took a pregnancy test at home.

gloss.en: an unfair dislike based on race, religion, or sex

words.example: He was a victim of racial prejudice.

gloss.en: happening before a more important event

words.example: After a few preliminary remarks, she began.

gloss.en: most important, famous or successful

words.example: He is one of the country's premier chefs.

gloss.en: an idea forming the basis of an argument

words.example: The basic premise of her argument is flawed.

gloss.en: an amount paid for an insurance policy

words.example: She pays a monthly premium for her insurance.

gloss.en: to tell someone to take a particular medicine

words.example: The doctor will prescribe some medicine.

gloss.en: a paper on which a doctor writes your medicine

words.example: The doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics.

gloss.en: at the present time; now

words.example: The crime is presently being investigated.

gloss.en: the act of keeping something in good condition

words.example: They care about the preservation of old buildings.

gloss.en: to lead or be in charge of a meeting

words.example: She will preside over the meeting today.

gloss.en: the job or term of being a president

words.example: He held the presidency for two terms.

gloss.en: connected with a president's role or work

words.example: The presidential election is next month.

gloss.en: respected and admired as important or high quality

words.example: She won a prestigious award for her work.

gloss.en: used to say something is probably true

words.example: Presumably this is where the accident happened.

gloss.en: to suppose something is true without proof

words.example: I presume they are very expensive.

gloss.en: to exist or be common at a time or place

words.example: Poor conditions prevail in local prisons.

gloss.en: the fact of being common at a time or place

words.example: There is an increase in the prevalence of smoking.

gloss.en: the act of stopping something bad from happening

words.example: The campaign focuses on crime prevention.

gloss.en: an animal that is hunted and eaten by another

words.example: The lion stalked its prey for hours.

gloss.en: most important; main

words.example: The principal reason for delay is lack of time.

gloss.en: selling a business so it is no longer government-owned

words.example: People feared privatization would cause job losses.

gloss.en: a special right or advantage for some people

words.example: Education should be a right, not a privilege.

gloss.en: a careful and complete investigation

words.example: The police launched a probe into the company.

gloss.en: difficult to deal with; full of problems

words.example: The situation is more problematic than we thought.

gloss.en: the process of using a court to settle a dispute

words.example: They began legal proceedings against the firm.

gloss.en: the money received from selling or an event

words.example: She sold her car and bought a piano with the proceeds.

gloss.en: treating raw material or food to change it

words.example: He works in the food processing industry.

gloss.en: a machine or company that processes things

words.example: The company is the largest dairy processor.

gloss.en: to officially and publicly announce something important

words.example: The president proclaimed a state of emergency.

gloss.en: making goods or crops, especially in large amounts

words.example: This is highly productive farming land.

gloss.en: the rate at which goods are produced

words.example: The factory saw increased productivity this year.

gloss.en: that makes or is likely to make money

words.example: They run a highly profitable business.

gloss.en: very great; felt or experienced very strongly

words.example: There are profound changes in the earth's climate.

gloss.en: an estimate of future figures based on now

words.example: They made a projection of future population figures.

gloss.en: important or well known

words.example: She is a prominent politician.

gloss.en: very obvious or easy to notice

words.example: He walked with a pronounced limp.

gloss.en: biased ideas used to gain political support

words.example: The leaflets were full of enemy propaganda.

gloss.en: an idea or plan suggested, especially in business

words.example: I'd like to put a business proposition to you.

gloss.en: to officially charge someone with a crime in court

words.example: The police decided not to prosecute.

gloss.en: the process of charging someone with a crime in court

words.example: Prosecution for a first minor offence is rare.

gloss.en: an official who charges and prosecutes people in court

words.example: The state prosecutor presented the evidence.

gloss.en: expected to do or become something

words.example: A prospective buyer came to see the house.

gloss.en: the state of being successful, especially financially

words.example: Our future prosperity depends on economic growth.

gloss.en: providing or intended to provide protection

words.example: Workers should wear full protective clothing.

gloss.en: a system of fixed rules for official occasions

words.example: His behaviour was a breach of protocol.

gloss.en: an area a country is divided into with local government

words.example: Canada is divided into ten provinces.

gloss.en: connected with a province and its local government

words.example: They voted in the provincial elections.

gloss.en: the act of supplying something needed

words.example: The city improved its housing provision.

gloss.en: to cause a particular reaction or effect

words.example: The announcement provoked a storm of protest.

gloss.en: relating to psychiatry or mental illnesses

words.example: He was treated for psychiatric disorders.

gloss.en: the regular beat of the heart felt in the body

words.example: The nurse felt a strong pulse in his wrist.

gloss.en: a machine that forces liquid, gas or air through

words.example: He filled the car at the petrol pump.

gloss.en: a hard hit made with the closed hand

words.example: He gave the man a punch in the face.

gloss.en: a question asking for information or expressing doubt

words.example: Our assistants will answer your queries.

gloss.en: a long search for something important

words.example: They began a quest for knowledge and truth.

gloss.en: a limited amount officially allowed

words.example: They introduced a strict import quota on grain.

gloss.en: a system using radio waves to locate objects

words.example: They located the ship by radar.

gloss.en: relating to basic, important parts; complete

words.example: There is a need for radical changes in education.

gloss.en: a feeling of violent anger hard to control

words.example: His face was dark with rage.

gloss.en: a short surprise attack on an enemy

words.example: The soldiers decided to launch a raid.

gloss.en: a large public meeting to support an idea

words.example: Thousands came to attend the rally.

gloss.en: a position on a scale showing how good something is

words.example: He improved his ranking this season.

gloss.en: the crime of forcing someone to have sex

words.example: He was charged with rape.

gloss.en: the relationship between two amounts shown as numbers

words.example: The school has a high teacher-student ratio.

gloss.en: based on reason rather than emotions

words.example: She made a rational decision after thinking it over.

gloss.en: a narrow line of light, heat or energy

words.example: The last ray of sun shone on the grass.

gloss.en: quickly and without difficulty

words.example: All ingredients are readily available locally.

gloss.en: the process of becoming aware of something

words.example: As realization dawned, he went pale.
